tool_input.content:
"""Base class for Parser mixins — declares shared state and helper shapes.

``Parser`` is recursive descent: rule methods call their neighbours through
``self`` rather than through a dispatch table.  When rule methods move to
mixin files, those ``self.X`` calls keep working at runtime because the final
``Parser`` class inherits all the mixins through its MRO.

Type checkers, however, see each mixin file in isolation.  This module
provides a single base class, :class:`ParserBase`, whose ``TYPE_CHECKING``
block declares every cross-cutting attribute and helper method that mixin
methods reference.  The declarations have no runtime effect — the real
implementations remain on :class:`Parser` (or on whichever mixin holds them
after later moves) and are reached through the normal MRO.

As subsequent moves consolidate helpers into their own mixin files, the
declarations migrate out of this module in lockstep.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
        ArgueChain,
        AxDef,
        BMachine,
        CaseAnalysis,
        Contents,
        Declaration,
        Document,
        DocumentItem,
        Expr,
        FreeType,
        GenDef,
        GivenType,
        HorizDef,
        InfruleBlock,
        LatexBlock,
        LineBreak,
        PageBreak,
        Paragraph,
        Part,
        PartsFormat,
        ProofNode,
        ProofTree,
        PureParagraph,
        RawLatexBlock,
        Schema,
        SchemaInclusion,
        SchemaText,
        Section,
        Solution,
        SyntaxBlock,
        TruthTable,
        Zed,
    )
    from txt2tex.lexer import Token


class ParserBase:
    """Type-only shape declaration for the composed ``Parser`` class.

    Every mixin in this package inherits from this class so that its methods
    type-check against the full parser interface.  The class has no runtime
    attributes or methods of its own — see the ``TYPE_CHECKING`` block below
    for what it declares to the type checker.
    """

    if TYPE_CHECKING:
        # --- Token stream + position state ---
        tokens: "list[Token]"
        pos: int
        last_token_end_column: int
        last_token_line: int

        # --- Context flags ---
        _parsing_schema_text: bool
        _in_comprehension_body: bool
        _in_schema_expr_context: bool
        _in_comparison_rhs: bool
        _current_quantifier_vars: set[str]
        _in_relational_context: bool

        # --- Token-cursor helpers (Move 21 → lexer_state.py) ---
        def _at_end(self) -> bool: ...
        def _current(self) -> "Token": ...
        def _advance(self) -> "Token": ...
        def _match(self, *kinds: str) -> bool: ...
        def _peek_ahead(self, offset: int = 1) -> "Token | None": ...
        def _skip_newlines(self) -> None: ...
        def _has_blank_line(self) -> bool: ...
        def _is_keyword_usable_as_identifier(self, token: "Token") -> bool: ...
        def _is_operand_start(self, token: "Token") -> bool: ...
        def _is_attr_name_token(self, token: "Token") -> bool: ...
        def _bracket_contains_slash(self) -> bool: ...
        def _scan_for_colon_before_newline(self) -> bool: ...
        def _scan_for_defs_after_brackets(self) -> bool: ...
        def _next_non_newline_is_cross_op(self) -> bool: ...
        def _reject_stray_slash(self, token: "Token") -> None: ...
        def _reject_reserved_decl_name(self, name: str, token: "Token") -> None: ...
        def _is_structural_token(self, token: "Token") -> bool: ...

        # --- Expression parser (Move 16) ---
        def _parse_expr(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_conditional(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_iff(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_implies(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_implies_rhs(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_or(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_and(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_unary(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_additive(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_range(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_multiplicative(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_comparison(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_relation(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_set_op(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_union(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_intersect(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_cross(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_postfix(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_atom(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_argument_list(self) -> "list[Expr]": ...
        def _parse_parenthesized_expr_or_tuple(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_set(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_set_comprehension_from_brace(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_set_predicate(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_set_expression(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_sequence_literal(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_bag_literal(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_quantifier(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_quantifier_continuation(
            self, quantifier: str, line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_quantifier_body(
            self, quantifier: str, line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_lambda(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_binding(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_binding_component(self) -> "tuple[str, Expr]": ...
        def _should_parse_space_separated_arg(self) -> bool: ...
        def _try_parse_guarded_cases(self) -> "Expr | None": ...

        # --- Type / algebra parsers (Moves 15, 17) ---
        def _parse_function_type(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_restrict(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_project(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_relation_rename(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_generic_instantiation(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_group_rhs(
            self, relation: "Expr", line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_group_regroup_rhs(
            self, relation: "Expr", line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_group_aggregate_rhs(
            self, relation: "Expr", line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_aggregator_clause(self) -> object: ...
        def _parse_ungroup_rhs(
            self, relation: "Expr", line: int, column: int
        ) -> "Expr": ...

        # --- Schema parsers (Move 13) ---
        def _parse_schema_pipe(self, lhs: "Expr") -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_compose(self, lhs: "Expr") -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_project_hide(self, lhs: "Expr") -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_text(self) -> "SchemaText": ...
        def _parse_schema_rename(self, lhs: "Expr") -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_rename_or_generic(self, lhs: "Expr") -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_horiz_def_generic_params(self) -> "list[str]": ...
        def _parse_horiz_def_rhs(self) -> "Expr": ...
        def _parse_schema_body(self) -> "tuple[list[Declaration | SchemaInclusion], list[list[Expr]]]": ...

        # --- Paragraph parsers (Move 12) ---
        def _parse_given_type(self) -> "GivenType": ...
        def _parse_free_type(self) -> "FreeType": ...
        def _parse_free_branch(self) -> object: ...
        def _parse_syntax_block(self) -> "SyntaxBlock": ...
        def _parse_abbreviation(self) -> object: ...
        def _parse_axdef(self) -> "AxDef": ...
        def _parse_gendef(self) -> "GenDef": ...
        def _parse_zed(self) -> "Zed": ...

        # --- Proof parsers (Move 14) ---
        def _parse_proof_tree(self) -> "ProofTree": ...
        def _parse_proof_node(self) -> "ProofNode": ...
        def _parse_case_analysis(self) -> "CaseAnalysis": ...
        def _parse_argue_chain(self) -> "ArgueChain": ...
        def _parse_infrule_block(self) -> "InfruleBlock": ...

        # --- Misc helpers ---
        def _parse_generic_params(self) -> "list[str]": ...
        def _parse_compound_identifier_name(self) -> str: ...
        def _parse_declaration_or_inclusion(
            self,
        ) -> "Declaration | SchemaInclusion": ...
        def _parse_predicate_groups(self) -> "list[list[Expr]]": ...
        def _parse_inclusion_generic_args(self) -> "list[Expr]": ...
        def _smart_join_justification(self, parts: "list[str]") -> str: ...
        def _parse_title_metadata(self) -> "tuple[str | None, str | None]": ...

        # --- Document item parsers (Moves 19, structural) ---
        def _parse_document_item(self) -> "DocumentItem": ...
        def _parse_section(self) -> "Section": ...
        def _parse_solution(self) -> "Solution": ...
        def _parse_part(self) -> "Part": ...
        def _parse_paragraph(self) -> "Paragraph": ...
        def _parse_paragraph_coalesced(self) -> "Paragraph": ...
        def _parse_pure_paragraph(self) -> "PureParagraph": ...
        def _parse_latex_block(self) -> "LatexBlock": ...
        def _parse_raw_latex_block(self) -> "RawLatexBlock": ...
        def _parse_b_block(self) -> "BMachine": ...
        def _parse_pagebreak(self) -> "PageBreak": ...
        def _parse_linebreak(self) -> "LineBreak": ...
        def _parse_contents(self) -> "Contents": ...
        def _parse_parts_directive(self) -> object: ...
        def _parse_parts_format(self) -> "PartsFormat": ...
        def _parse_truth_table(self) -> "TruthTable": ...
        def _parse_identifier_document_item(self) -> "DocumentItem": ...
        def _parse_bracket_document_item(self) -> "DocumentItem": ...
        def _parse_bibliography_metadata(self) -> None: ...

import ast, sys
src = sys.stdin.read()
# Quick approximation: AST names start with uppercase. Find module-level identifiers used.
import re
names = set(re.findall(r'\\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z]+)\\b', src))
# Filter to ones that look like AST node names (not all-caps constants, not common words)
candidates = {n for n in names if not n.isupper() and len(n) > 2}
# Filter further to known AST nodes
ast_nodes = '''Abbreviation AxDef GenDef GivenType FreeType Zed SyntaxBlock SyntaxDefinition FreeBranch Document DocumentItem Expr Identifier Declaration SchemaInclusion SequenceLiteral Schema HorizDef SchemaText'''.split()
present = sorted(c for c in ast_nodes if c in candidates)
print(present)

from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

SRC = Path("src/txt2tex/parser.py")

# --- Move 12: paragraphs.py ---
PARA_BLOCKS = [
    (4979, 5276),  # _parse_given_type + _parse_free_branch + _parse_free_type + _parse_syntax_block
    (5710, 6026),  # _parse_compound_identifier_name + _parse_abbreviation + _parse_axdef + _parse_gendef + _parse_zed
]

# --- Move 13: schemas.py ---
SCHEMA_BLOCKS = [
    (1147, 1162),   # _parse_schema_rename_or_generic
    (1198, 1296),   # _parse_schema_rename
    (5307, 5675),   # _parse_horiz_def_generic_params + _parse_schema_pipe + _parse_schema_compose + _parse_schema_project_hide + _parse_horiz_def + _parse_horiz_def_rhs + _parse_schema_text
    (6028, 6112),   # _parse_schema
]

# --- Move 14: proofs.py ---
PROOF_BLOCKS = [
    (1450, 1481),   # _smart_join_justification
    (1672, 1871),   # _parse_argue_chain + _parse_infrule_block
    (6114, 6367),   # _parse_proof_tree + _parse_proof_node + _parse_case_analysis (to EOF)
]

PARA_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for Z paragraph constructs.

Covers: GivenType, FreeType (and FreeBranch), SyntaxBlock,
Abbreviation, AxDef, GenDef, Zed.  Includes the small helper
``_parse_compound_identifier_name`` that is consumed only by
``_parse_abbreviation``.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    Abbreviation,
    AxDef,
    Declaration,
    Document,
    DocumentItem,
    Expr,
    FreeBranch,
    FreeType,
    GenDef,
    GivenType,
    Identifier,
    SchemaInclusion,
    SequenceLiteral,
    SyntaxBlock,
    SyntaxDefinition,
    Zed,
)
from txt2tex.lexer import TokenType
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase


class _ParagraphsParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for Z paragraph constructs."""

'''

SCHEMA_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for schema constructs.

Covers: Schema, HorizDef (with helper ``_parse_horiz_def_rhs`` and
``_parse_horiz_def_generic_params``), inline schema text
``_parse_schema_text``, schema-calculus operators
(``_parse_schema_pipe``, ``_parse_schema_compose``,
``_parse_schema_project_hide``), and the dispatcher
``_parse_schema_rename_or_generic`` plus the renaming form
``_parse_schema_rename``.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    Declaration,
    Document,
    Expr,
    HorizDef,
    Identifier,
    Schema,
    SchemaCompose,
    SchemaHide,
    SchemaInclusion,
    SchemaPipe,
    SchemaProject,
    SchemaRename,
    SchemaText,
)
from txt2tex.lexer import TokenType
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase


class _SchemasParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for schema constructs."""

'''

PROOF_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for proof constructs.

Covers: ProofTree, ProofNode, CaseAnalysis, ArgueChain
(ARGUE / EQUIV / EQUAL), InfruleBlock.  Includes the small helper
``_smart_join_justification`` that is shared among the proof-rule
methods.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    ArgueChain,
    ArgueStep,
    CaseAnalysis,
    CaseStep,
    Expr,
    Identifier,
    InfruleBlock,
    ProofNode,
    ProofTree,
)
from txt2tex.lexer import TokenType
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase


class _ProofsParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for proof constructs."""

'''


def extract_chunks(lines: list[str], blocks: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> str:
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for start, end in blocks:
        chunk = "".join(lines[start - 1 : end])
        chunks.append(chunk)
    return "".join(chunks).rstrip() + "\n"


def write_files() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/paragraphs.py").write_text(
        PARA_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, PARA_BLOCKS)
    )
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/schemas.py").write_text(
        SCHEMA_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, SCHEMA_BLOCKS)
    )
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/proofs.py").write_text(
        PROOF_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, PROOF_BLOCKS)
    )
    print("wrote paragraphs.py, schemas.py, proofs.py")


def delete_blocks() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    keep = [True] * len(lines)
    for start, end in (*PARA_BLOCKS, *SCHEMA_BLOCKS, *PROOF_BLOCKS):
        for i in range(start - 1, end):
            keep[i] = False
    out = [ln for ln, k in zip(lines, keep) if k]
    SRC.write_text("".join(out))
    print(f"parser.py: {len(out)} lines")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    write_files()
    delete_blocks()

    token: Token

        """Initialize parser error with token position."""

        self.message = message
        self.token = token

"""Extract Batch 7: parser_pkg/{algebra,expressions,types}.py."""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

SRC = Path("src/txt2tex/parser.py")

# Move 15: algebra.py — relational-algebra parser rules.
ALG_BLOCKS = [
    (3523, 3700),  # _parse_group_rhs through _parse_ungroup_rhs (group/aggregate cluster)
    (4131, 4314),  # _parse_restrict, _parse_project, _parse_relation_rename, _is_attr_name_token
]

# Move 17: types.py — type-expression parsers.
TYPES_BLOCKS = [
    (1125, 1158),  # _parse_generic_instantiation
    (3161, 3195),  # _parse_function_type
    (4635, 4666),  # _parse_generic_params (to EOF)
]

# Move 16: expressions.py — everything else (all expression-grammar rules).
EXPR_BLOCKS = [
    (1500, 1514),  # _parse_expr
    (1515, 1622),  # _parse_conditional, _parse_iff
    (1623, 1687),  # _parse_implies, _parse_implies_rhs
    (1688, 1769),  # _parse_or, _parse_and
    (1770, 1862),  # _parse_unary, _parse_additive, _parse_range
    (1863, 1918),  # _parse_multiplicative, _is_operand_start
    (1919, 2044),  # _should_parse_space_separated_arg
    (2045, 2384),  # _parse_quantifier, _parse_schema_quantifier_body
    (2385, 2533),  # _parse_quantifier_continuation
    (2534, 2696),  # _parse_lambda, _parse_binding
    (2697, 2746),  # _parse_binding_component
    (2747, 2981),  # _parse_set, _parse_set_comprehension_from_brace
    (2982, 3002),  # _parse_set_predicate, _parse_set_expression
    (3003, 3067),  # _parse_comparison
    (3068, 3160),  # _try_parse_guarded_cases
    (3196, 3284),  # _parse_relation, _parse_set_op, _parse_union
    (3285, 3522),  # _parse_cross, _next_non_newline_is_cross_op
    (3701, 4065),  # _parse_intersect, _parse_postfix
    (4066, 4130),  # _parse_parenthesized_expr_or_tuple
    (4315, 4605),  # _parse_atom, _parse_argument_list, _parse_sequence_literal, _parse_bag_literal
]

ALG_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for relational-algebra constructs.

Covers: ``_parse_restrict`` (sigma), ``_parse_project`` (pi),
``_parse_relation_rename`` (R[B/A]), the GROUP family
(``_parse_group_rhs``, ``_parse_group_regroup_rhs``,
``_parse_group_aggregate_rhs``, ``_parse_aggregator_clause``,
``_parse_ungroup_rhs``), and the ``_is_attr_name_token`` helper
consumed by ``_parse_relation_rename``.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    AggregatorClause,
    Aggregator,
    Expr,
    Group,
    GroupAggregate,
    Identifier,
    Project,
    RelationRename,
    Restrict,
    Ungroup,
)
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import Token, TokenType


class _AlgebraParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for relational-algebra constructs."""

'''

TYPES_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for type expressions and generic parameters.

Covers: ``_parse_function_type``, ``_parse_generic_instantiation``,
``_parse_generic_params``.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import Expr, FunctionType, GenericInstantiation
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import TokenType


class _TypesParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for type expressions and generic parameters."""

'''

EXPR_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for general expression grammar (recursive descent).

Covers the full Pratt-style expression grammar from ``_parse_expr``
down to ``_parse_atom``: conditionals, iff/implies, or/and, unary,
arithmetic, range, comparison, relation/set ops, union, cross,
intersect, postfix, plus quantifier/lambda/binding/set-comprehension
parsers and atom literals (sequence, bag).

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Literal

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    BagLiteral,
    BinaryOp,
    Binding,
    Conditional,
    Expr,
    FunctionApp,
    GuardedBranch,
    GuardedCases,
    Identifier,
    Lambda,
    Number,
    Quantifier,
    Range,
    RelationalImage,
    SchemaBinding,
    SchemaText,
    SequenceLiteral,
    SetComprehension,
    SetLiteral,
    StringLit,
    Subscript,
    Superscript,
    Theta,
    Tuple,
    TupleProjection,
    UnaryOp,
)
from txt2tex.constants import PROSE_WORDS
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import Token, TokenType


class _ExpressionsParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: full expression-grammar parser."""

'''


def extract_chunks(lines: list[str], blocks: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> str:
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for start, end in blocks:
        chunks.append("".join(lines[start - 1 : end]))
    return "".join(chunks).rstrip() + "\n"


def write_files() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/algebra.py").write_text(
        ALG_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, ALG_BLOCKS)
    )
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/types.py").write_text(
        TYPES_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, TYPES_BLOCKS)
    )
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/expressions.py").write_text(
        EXPR_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, EXPR_BLOCKS)
    )
    print("wrote algebra.py, types.py, expressions.py")


def delete_blocks() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    keep = [True] * len(lines)
    for start, end in (*ALG_BLOCKS, *TYPES_BLOCKS, *EXPR_BLOCKS):
        for i in range(start - 1, end):
            keep[i] = False
    out = [ln for ln, k in zip(lines, keep) if k]
    SRC.write_text("".join(out))
    print(f"parser.py: {len(out)} lines")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    write_files()
    delete_blocks()

"""Extract Batch 8: parser_pkg/text_blocks.py."""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

SRC = Path("src/txt2tex/parser.py")

TEXT_BLOCKS = [
    (452, 770),   # _parse_paragraph through _parse_parts_format (contiguous)
    (1251, 1438), # _parse_section, _parse_solution, _parse_part, _parse_truth_table (contiguous)
]

TEXT_HEADER = '''"""Parser rules for text and presentation constructs.

Covers: Section, Solution, Part, Paragraph (TEXT:), PureParagraph
(PURETEXT:), LatexBlock (LATEX:), RawLatexBlock, BMachine (B:),
LineBreak, PageBreak, Contents, BibliographyMetadata, PartsFormat
(plus its directive parser), PartsDirective, and TruthTable.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re

from txt2tex.ast_nodes import (
    BibliographyMetadata,
    BMachine,
    Contents,
    DocumentItem,
    Expr,
    LatexBlock,
    LineBreak,
    PageBreak,
    Paragraph,
    Part,
    PartsFormat,
    PureParagraph,
    RawLatexBlock,
    Section,
    Solution,
    TruthTable,
)
from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import TokenType


class _TextBlocksParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: rules for text and presentation constructs."""

'''


def extract_chunks(lines: list[str], blocks: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> str:
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for start, end in blocks:
        chunks.append("".join(lines[start - 1 : end]))
    return "".join(chunks).rstrip() + "\n"


def main() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)

"""Extract Batch 9: parser_pkg/{lexer_state,errors}.py."""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

SRC = Path("src/txt2tex/parser.py")

# Move 21: lexer_state.py — token cursor helpers.
LEXER_BLOCKS = [
    (503, 530),  # _at_end, _current, _advance, _match, _peek_ahead
    (548, 572),  # _skip_newlines, _has_blank_line
    (747, 767),  # _bracket_contains_slash
]

# Move 22: errors.py — error-construction helpers.
ERR_BLOCKS = [
    (532, 546),  # _reject_stray_slash
    (646, 661),  # _reject_reserved_decl_name
]

LEXER_HEADER = '''"""Token-cursor helpers for the recursive-descent parser.

Covers: ``_at_end``, ``_current``, ``_advance``, ``_match``,
``_peek_ahead``, ``_skip_newlines``, ``_has_blank_line``,
``_bracket_contains_slash``.  Every rule method in the parser_pkg
reaches the token stream through these helpers.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import Token, TokenType


class _LexerStateParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: token-cursor helpers."""

'''

ERR_HEADER = '''"""Error-construction helpers for the recursive-descent parser.

Covers: ``_reject_stray_slash`` and ``_reject_reserved_decl_name``.
``ParserError`` itself lives in ``parser_pkg/_base.py`` because the
mixins consume it from there.

This mixin is composed into :class:`Parser` via multiple inheritance.
Method bodies are byte-identical to their counterparts in the
pre-refactor monolithic ``parser.py``; only their file location has
changed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from txt2tex.parser_pkg._base import ParserBase, ParserError
from txt2tex.tokens import Token, TokenType


class _ErrorsParser(ParserBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedClass]
    """Mixin: error-construction helpers."""

'''


def extract_chunks(lines: list[str], blocks: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> str:
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for start, end in blocks:
        chunks.append("".join(lines[start - 1 : end]))
    return "".join(chunks).rstrip() + "\n"


def main() -> None:
    lines = SRC.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/lexer_state.py").write_text(
        LEXER_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, LEXER_BLOCKS)
    )
    Path("src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/errors.py").write_text(
        ERR_HEADER + extract_chunks(lines, ERR_BLOCKS)
    )
    keep = [True] * len(lines)
    for start, end in (*LEXER_BLOCKS, *ERR_BLOCKS):
        for i in range(start - 1, end):
            keep[i] = False
    out = [ln for ln, k in zip(lines, keep) if k]
    SRC.write_text("".join(out))
    print(f"lexer_state.py + errors.py written; parser.py: {len(out)} lines")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

### Phase 1 (✅ done, 2026-05-25) — Family-line split of `latex_gen.py` and `parser.py`

**Status.** Delivered on branch `refactor/phase1-family-split`,
twelve commits (Move 0 + nine batches + docs + tooling).  Every
batch verified at three gates: `make check`, `make test-e2e`
(159/159 fixtures byte-identical), and `make refactor-diff`
(190/190 examples + tests/bugs byte-identical vs main).

**Final line counts.**

| File | main (baseline) | after Phase 1 | reduction |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/txt2tex/latex_gen.py` | 6,864 | 598 | 91.3% |
| `src/txt2tex/parser.py` | 6,367 | 846 | 86.7% |

The two former monoliths are now orchestrator shells.  Handler
implementations live under `src/txt2tex/codegen/` (11 mixin files,
~7.4k lines) and `src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/` (9 mixin files plus a
`_base.py` shape declaration, ~6.9k lines).  No file in `src/txt2tex/`
exceeds 2,800 lines; the largest are `codegen/text_pipeline.py`
(the ASCII-to-LaTeX conversion subsystem) and
`parser_pkg/expressions.py` (the full recursive-descent grammar).

**Scope.** Move handlers from the monolithic files into the
directory structure above.  Behaviour does not change: every test
that passes today must pass after the move, byte-for-byte on the
e2e fixtures.  The `LaTeXGenerator` and `Parser` classes remain;
their methods are physically relocated and `from ... import ...`
ties them back together.

## Summary

Phase 1 of [docs/DESIGN-ROADMAP.md](docs/DESIGN-ROADMAP.md): split
the two monolithic generator/parser files into directory trees
organised by construct family.  Behaviour is unchanged — every
`.tex` output across the `examples/` + `tests/bugs/` corpus is
byte-identical against `main` (190 / 190).

### Numbers

| File | main | this PR | reduction |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/txt2tex/latex_gen.py` | 6,864 | 598 | 91.3% |
| `src/txt2tex/parser.py`    | 6,367 | 846 | 86.7% |

Handlers now live under:

- `src/txt2tex/codegen/` — 11 mixin files (paragraphs, schemas,
  proofs, algebra, expressions, types, bindings, text-blocks, text
  pipeline, overflow, fuzz-routing, paren-policy) + `_dispatch.py`
  base.
- `src/txt2tex/parser_pkg/` — 9 mixin files (paragraphs, schemas,
  proofs, algebra, expressions, types, text-blocks, lexer-state,
  errors) + `_base.py` type-shape.

### Discipline

Every method body is a Fowler Move-Method — byte-identical to its
pre-refactor source.  Mixin classes are composed into
`LaTeXGenerator` and `Parser` via multiple inheritance; the
`@singledispatchmethod` registry and the recursive-descent call
graph keep working through the MRO.

### Verification — three gates, every commit

- `make check` — lint + markdown + format + mypy (strict) +
  pyright (strict) + 4,700 unit tests.
- `make test-e2e` — 159 / 159 `examples/**/*.tex` fixtures
  byte-identical.
- `make refactor-diff` — **new gate added in this PR.**  190 / 190
  inputs across `examples/` + `tests/bugs/` produce `.tex` output
  byte-identical to `main`.  See `scripts/refactor_diff.py` and
  `scripts/refactor_diff_vs_ref.sh`.

### Commits

13 commits, each independently revertible:

1. Move 0 — dispatch infrastructure + smoke-test mixin.
2. Batch 1 — codegen paragraphs / schemas / proofs.
3. Tooling — `make refactor-diff` IO-contract gate.
4. Batch 2 — codegen algebra / expressions / types.
5. Batch 3 — codegen bindings / text-blocks / overflow.
6. Batch 4 — codegen fuzz-routing / paren-policy.
7. Docs — Phase 1A close-out batch in the plan.
8. Batch 5 — codegen atoms + text pipeline (Phase 1A close-out).
9. Batch 6 — parser paragraphs / schemas / proofs (Phase 1B open).
10. Batch 7 — parser algebra / expressions / types.
11. Batch 8 — parser text-blocks.
12. Batch 9 — parser lexer-state / errors (Phase 1B close-out).
13. Docs — mark Phase 1 done.

### Scope

This PR is structural only.  No behaviour change, no new public
API, no test edits, no CLI surface change.  Subsequent phases
(paren-policy extraction, rendered-tree IR) build on this foundation.

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` clean.
- [x] `make test-e2e` 159 / 159 byte-identical.
- [x] `make refactor-diff` 190 / 190 byte-identical vs main.
- [ ] Bot review (Copilot/Bugbot) addressed.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

   so capture and verify produce the same key on Windows and POSIX.

tool_input.new_string:
def generate_one(txt: Path, dst: Path) -> tuple[Path, bool]:
    """Run txt2tex on `txt`, write output to `dst`.  Return (dst, ok).

    The contract is byte-equality of the generated ``.tex`` file.  We
    therefore decide success on file presence, not on the subprocess
    exit code: ``txt2tex --tex-only`` exits non-zero when fuzz emits
    a type-check warning even though the ``.tex`` was generated
    correctly, and that ``.tex`` is exactly the artifact we want to
    compare.  Only when no file was produced do we record a sentinel
    so that verify can detect a *change* in rejection status.
    """
    dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    uv = shutil.which("uv")
    if uv is None:
        print("ERROR: uv not found on PATH", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    subprocess.run(
        [
            uv,
            "run",
            "txt2tex",
            str(txt),
            "--tex-only",
            "-o",
            str(dst),
        ],
        check=False,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        cwd=str(ROOT),
    )
    if not dst.exists():
        # Genuine rejection — txt2tex declined to produce any output.
        dst.write_text(FAILURE_SENTINEL)
        return dst, False
    return dst, True

    """Run txt2tex on `txt`, write output to `dst`.  Return (dst, ok)."""

    uv = shutil.which("uv")
    if uv is None:
        print("ERROR: uv not found on PATH", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            uv,
            "run",
            "txt2tex",
            str(txt),
            "--tex-only",
            "-o",
            str(dst),
        ],
        check=False,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        cwd=str(ROOT),
    )
    if result.returncode != 0 or not dst.exists():
        # Record the failure so verify can detect a *change* in failure status.
        dst.write_text(FAILURE_SENTINEL)
        return dst, False
    return dst, True

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(refactor-diff): decide success on .tex presence, not subprocess exit code

Follow-up to PR #51, addressing a second bug in
scripts/refactor_diff.py that was exposed by running the now-fixed
gate against v1.3.1.

`txt2tex --tex-only` returns exit=1 when fuzz emits a type-check
*warning* even though the .tex was generated correctly.  The script
treated any non-zero exit as a rejection and wrote the
`<<txt2tex did not produce .tex output>>` sentinel — so the .tex
that was on disk was discarded and never compared.  Both capture
and verify wrote the sentinel for those inputs, producing a
trivial sentinel-vs-sentinel "match".

Affected inputs in the corpus (the 10 previously reported as
"failed/rejected"):
- examples/06_definitions/axdef_demo.txt
- examples/10_schemas/schema_rename.txt
- examples/user_guide/30_composition.txt
- tests/bugs/bug3_compound_id.txt
- tests/bugs/bug3_test_simple.txt
- (plus 5 genuine bug-regression tests in tests/bugs/ that
  legitimately produce no output)

Three of those are `examples/` fixtures that test-e2e already
verifies, so the overall byte-equality claim was still grounded
in test-e2e.  The five `tests/bugs/` cases above were *not*
verified by anything — they were the marginal added value of
`make refactor-diff` and they were silently sentinel-matched.

Fix: drop the `result.returncode != 0` clause; decide success
purely on `dst.exists()`.  The .tex file is the artifact the gate
is meant to compare; the subprocess exit code carries downstream
fuzz/latexmk warnings that are not part of the contract.

Re-ran `make refactor-diff REF=v1.3.1` after the fix:

  capture: 185 ok, 5 failed/rejected, total 190
  PASS: 190 inputs match baseline

The 5 still-rejected inputs are intentional negative-regression
tests in tests/bugs/ that verify the parser refuses malformed
input (names start with `bug1_prose_period`,
`feature_bag_syntax_in_free_types`,
`regression_in_*`).  Sentinel-vs-sentinel is the correct
semantic for those.

So Phase 1 is genuinely byte-clean against v1.3.1: 185 generated
.tex files are byte-identical, 5 inputs reject in both versions.
EOF
)"

gh pr create --title "fix(refactor-diff): decide success on .tex presence, not subprocess exit code" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Follow-up to #51.  The `make refactor-diff` gate had a second bug
on top of the ROOT-cwd issue that #51 fixed: `txt2tex --tex-only`
exits non-zero when fuzz emits a type-check *warning* even though
the `.tex` was generated correctly.  The script treated any
non-zero exit as a rejection and wrote a sentinel — so the real
`.tex` was discarded and the comparison degenerated into
sentinel-vs-sentinel.

This was exposed by running the now-correct gate against
`v1.3.1`:

```
capture: 180 ok, 10 failed/rejected, total 190
PASS: 190 inputs match baseline
```

Five of those "rejected" inputs were `examples/` fixtures that
test-e2e already verifies, so the overall byte-equality claim
still held — but the marginal value of `make refactor-diff` over
test-e2e (the `tests/bugs/` cases) was silently sentinel-matching.

## Fix

Decide success purely on `dst.exists()`.  The `.tex` file is the
artifact the gate is meant to compare; the subprocess exit code
carries downstream fuzz/latexmk warnings that are not part of the
contract.

## Verification

After the fix:

```
$ make refactor-diff REF=v1.3.1
capture: 185 ok, 5 failed/rejected, total 190
PASS: 190 inputs match baseline
```

The remaining 5 rejections are intentional negative-regression
tests in `tests/bugs/` that verify the parser refuses malformed
input (`bug1_prose_period`, `feature_bag_syntax_in_free_types`,
three `regression_in_*` cases).  Sentinel-vs-sentinel is the
correct semantic there.

So Phase 1 is genuinely byte-clean against `v1.3.1`: 185
generated `.tex` files byte-identical, 5 inputs reject in both
versions.

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` clean.
- [x] `make refactor-diff REF=v1.3.1` PASS 190/190 (185 real
  comparisons, 5 intentional rejections).
- [ ] Address bot review.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
